summary: Tight Stephen‑Iko sequence, soaring Estimated Prophet, fiery jams
blurb: The show shines with a legendary St. Stephen > Iko > The Other One segue and a blistering Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World transition. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, while the rhythm section drives powerful jams on Bertha, Jack‑A‑Roe and Not Fade Away.
review: May 15, 1977 at St. Louis Arena captures one of spring ’77’s most electrifying nights. The first set bursts open with a swinging Bertha that segues into a flawless Good Lovin' before the band launches into a tight Row Jimmy and a joyous Jack‑A‑Roe. The real highlight arrives in the second set: a seamless Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World combo that rolls straight into a mythic St. Stephen > Iko > The Other One sequence, followed by a thunderous Not Fade Away into Sugar Magnolia. Throughout, Jerry’s guitar soars—wah‑filled on Estimated and dark‑star‑like on Not Fade Away—while Phil anchors with a deep, thumping bass line that drives the jams. Keith’s keys add texture without ever getting in the way, and the dual drummers lay down big, propulsive rhythms that keep the energy sky‑high to the encore’s Uncle John’s Band.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings are marked positive and one mixed, with no negative sentiment. Reviewers consistently praise the Stephen>Iko>Other One segue, Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World transition, and overall fire in Jerry’s playing, indicating strong positive consensus.

best_recording.reason: Exceptional soundboard clarity, praised as “dialed‑in” and “killer sound,” capturing every highlight without dropouts.

key_highlights: Legendary St. Stephen > Iko > The Other One segue
Blistering Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World transition
Ferocious Dancin' In The Street and Not Fade Away jams

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers scorching, wah‑filled solos on Estimated and fierce lead work on Not Fade Away; his guitar is consistently described as ‘on fire’ and ‘dark‑star‑like.’
band_performance.Phil: Locks into a deep, thumping bass groove that propels the jams, especially during the Iko transition and the high‑energy second set.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid vocal harmonies, notably with Donna Jean on Bertha and Jack‑A‑Roe; his rhythm guitar underpins the band’s drive without missing a beat.
band_performance.Mickey: Together with Bill, lays down big, propulsive drum work that fuels the high‑octane moments like Samson & Delilah and the closing Not Fade Away.
band_performance.Billy: Offers powerful, “big drums” throughout, especially evident on the dynamic transitions and the intense second‑set flow.
